title: Signal Pulse EA
subtitle: Multi-Timeframe Bollinger Bands + Stochastic Confluence — M15/M30/H1 Alignment
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: EXTRACT — multi-timeframe confirmation architecture. Replace BB+Stochastic with zone+EMA+ADX.

series_evolution.part_5: RSI + BB + ATR confluence on single timeframe
series_evolution.part_7: Bollinger Bands + Stochastic confluence across THREE timeframes (M15, M30, H1). The key advancement: multi-timeframe alignment. Parts 5 and 7 together establish that good signals require both indicator convergence AND timeframe convergence.

core_concept.name: Multi-Timeframe Confluence Gate
core_concept.description: Require the SAME condition to be true on ALL three timeframes simultaneously. A signal on M15 alone is noise. A signal on M15, M30, AND H1 simultaneously is a genuine structural moment.
buy_conditions: M15 close < M15 BB_lower  AND  M15 Stochastic_K < 5
M30 close < M30 BB_lower  AND  M30 Stochastic_K < 5
H1  close < H1  BB_lower  AND  H1  Stochastic_K < 5
sell_conditions: M15 close > M15 BB_upper  AND  M15 Stochastic_K > 95
M30 close > M30 BB_upper  AND  M30 Stochastic_K > 95
H1  close > H1  BB_upper  AND  H1  Stochastic_K > 95

extreme_thresholds.buy_k: < 5 (not just < 20 — author uses extreme oversold only)
extreme_thresholds.rationale: Extreme thresholds reduce signal frequency but radically increase quality. Fewer, better signals.

core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: This is exactly our M15/M1 two-timeframe approach but extended to three. Our M15=structure, M1=timing maps to: H1=macro context, M15=structure, M1=entry. The concept is identical.

signal_offset.description: Vertical offset for arrow placement above/below candle

test_bars.description: How many historical bars to check on init

min_arrow_distance.description: Minimum distance between arrows to prevent duplicates

steps.name: fetch_indicators_all_timeframes
steps.description: Get BB upper/lower and Stochastic K for each of the 3 timeframes

steps.name: check_arrow_proximity
steps.description: Prevent drawing overlapping arrows. Check if a recent signal exists within min_arrow_distance.

steps.name: draw_signal_arrow
steps.description: If signal and no nearby arrow: draw green up-arrow (buy) or red down-arrow (sell) on M15 chart

steps.name: log_signal
steps.description: Append signal to signalTimes[], signalPrices[], signalBuySignals[] arrays for historical tracking

no_sl_tp.note: Part 7 generates entry signals only — NO SL or TP calculation. Author defers trade management to later parts. This is a pure signal filter.
no_sl_tp.contrast_with_part_5: Part 5 had ATR-based SL/TP. Part 7 reverts to signal-only. The series is building components independently before assembling them.
no_sl_tp.implication_for_creviaderiv: Our EntrySignal object carries SL/TP from _calculate_precision_sl() — we don't have this split. We always compute SL/TP when we generate a signal.

multi_timeframe_architecture.key_insight: Each timeframe needs its own indicator instances. You cannot share a single BB handle across timeframes — each timeframe produces different BB values.

our_implementation.m1_data: Used for timing (EntryEngine): EMA3 cross, ADX, departure candle
our_implementation.separation: M15=WHERE (structure), M1=WHEN (entry). Two timeframes instead of three, but same architecture.

data_fetching_pattern.mql5: iCopy() with specific PERIOD_ constant to get HTF bars

components_to_build.name: StochasticCalculator
components_to_build.contract: stochastic(highs, lows, closes, k_period, d_period, slowing) -> {K: List[float], D: List[float]}

components_to_build.name: MultiTimeframeDataFetcher
components_to_build.note: Wraps BrokerDataAdapter. Returns dict keyed by timeframe string.

components_to_build.name: ArrowManager
components_to_build.note: Deduplication logic: check if prior arrow within min_distance. Returns False (don't draw) if too close.

keep: Multi-timeframe confirmation requirement — excellent false-signal filter
Extreme thresholds (K<5, K>95) — much tighter than standard 20/80, far fewer but higher quality signals
Signal deduplication (arrow proximity check) — prevents double-counting the same signal
Signal history log (times, prices, directions) — foundation for backtesting and analysis
discard: Bollinger Bands + Stochastic as primary triggers — replaced by zone touch in CreviaDeriv
Stochastic K<5/K>95 thresholds — replaced by ADX rising and EMA3 crossover gates
MQL5 chart arrows (ObjectCreate) — replaced by ChartRenderer.draw_arrow
Three fixed timeframes (M15/M30/H1) — our system uses M15+M1 pair, more focused
improve: Replace BB+Stochastic with supply/demand zone proximity as primary trigger
Replace multi-TF indicator alignment with M15 structure + M1 timing distinction
Add SL/TP to this signal — Part 7 generates arrows with no risk levels
Use configurable timeframe pairs, not hardcoded 3-TF stack
Add VWV (velocity) gate — no volume confirmation here

creviaderiv_insights.mtf_confirmation_validated: Multi-timeframe confirmation is industry standard. Our M15+M1 architecture is correct. This part validates we should NOT use a single timeframe for both structure and entry.
creviaderiv_insights.extreme_thresholds_lesson: K<5 and K>95 (not 20/80) produce fewer, higher quality signals. Our ADX > 20 threshold (not > 0) follows the same principle — require meaningful movement.
creviaderiv_insights.signal_deduplication: The ArrowManager proximity check maps to our single-signal-per-zone logic in EntryEngine — once a signal fires for a zone, we don't re-fire on the same zone interaction.

article_figures.title: Buy Condition Diagram
article_figures.insight: Shows three-timeframe stack with all three BB lower-touch + Stoch<5 conditions highlighted simultaneously.

article_figures.title: Sell Condition Diagram
article_figures.insight: Mirror of buy condition. Upper BB touch across all three TFs with Stoch>95.

article_figures.title: Algorithm Flow Chart
article_figures.insight: Decision tree: Fetch TF1 → Check buy/sell → Fetch TF2 → Check → Fetch TF3 → Check → Signal if all pass.

article_figures.title: Pulse Trader Signal Result
article_figures.insight: Chart showing green and red arrows. Arrows appear sparsely — confirms that extreme thresholds filter most signals.

article_figures.title: Test Result Animation (GIF)
article_figures.insight: Animated test showing arrows appearing in real time. Visually confirms signals align with actual BB+Stoch extremes.
